phases.name: Legacy Assessment & Knowledge Transfer
objectives: Catalog all IBMi green screen applications, RPG programs, CL scripts, and DDS files
Interview SMEs to document undocumented business rules embedded in legacy code
Map data flows between IBMi subsystems and external healthcare integrations (HL7, X12)
Establish knowledge transfer sessions with retiring IBMi developers
deliverables: Legacy application inventory with complexity scores
Business rules catalog extracted from RPG/CL source analysis
Data flow diagrams for all IBMi subsystems
Knowledge transfer recordings and runbooks
success_criteria: 100% of active RPG programs cataloged with complexity ratings
All critical business rules documented and validated by SMEs
Data lineage traced for patient, claims, and scheduling subsystems
risks: Tribal knowledge loss if key IBMi personnel depart before transfer completes
Undocumented interfaces between RPG programs may be missed in inventory

phases.name: Foundation & Infrastructure
objectives: Provision target cloud/hybrid infrastructure with HIPAA-compliant configurations
Establish CI/CD pipelines, IaC templates, and container orchestration
Configure database infrastructure (PostgreSQL/Aurora) with encryption at rest and in transit
Set up observability stack (logging, metrics, tracing, alerting)
deliverables: Infrastructure-as-Code repository (Terraform/Pulumi)
CI/CD pipeline definitions with security scanning gates
Database instances provisioned with schema migration tooling
Monitoring dashboards and alerting rules
success_criteria: Infrastructure passes HIPAA configuration audit
CI/CD pipeline deploys to dev environment in under 15 minutes
Database failover tested with RPO < 1 minute, RTO < 5 minutes
dependencies: P-001 legacy inventory informs infrastructure sizing
risks: HIPAA compliance requirements may delay infrastructure approval
Network connectivity between on-prem IBMi and cloud environment may introduce latency

phases.name: IAM & Patient Registration MVP
objectives: Implement identity and access management with RBAC aligned to healthcare roles
Migrate patient registration module as first modernized workload
Establish API gateway with FHIR R4 endpoints for patient demographics
Validate data migration pipeline with patient master data
deliverables: IAM service with SSO, MFA, and role-based access policies
Patient Registration microservice with FHIR R4 API
Data migration scripts for patient demographic records
Integration test suite covering registration workflows
success_criteria: IAM supports all 12 legacy user roles with equivalent permissions
Patient Registration handles 100% of legacy workflows identified in P-001
Data migration achieves 99.99% accuracy on patient demographic fields
API response times under 200ms at p95 for registration lookups
risks: Patient data migration may surface data quality issues in legacy DB2/400 tables
FHIR R4 mapping from proprietary IBMi data structures may require custom transformers

phases.name: Clinical Modules Development
objectives: Modernize scheduling, order entry, clinical documentation, and pharmacy modules
Implement HL7 FHIR interfaces for clinical data exchange
Build clinical decision support integration points
Develop reporting and analytics layer for clinical data
deliverables: Scheduling Service with provider calendar and patient self-scheduling
Order Entry Service (CPOE) with CDS hooks
Clinical Documentation Service with structured and unstructured note support
Pharmacy Module with e-prescribing (NCPDP SCRIPT) integration
Clinical analytics dashboard
success_criteria: All four clinical modules pass functional equivalence testing against legacy screens
HL7 FHIR interfaces certified against ONC requirements
Clinical workflows complete in equal or fewer steps than legacy green screen
E-prescribing passes Surescripts certification testing
dependencies: P-003 IAM and patient registration must be stable
P-002 infrastructure scaled for clinical workloads
risks: Clinical module complexity may exceed estimates due to undocumented business rules
HL7 FHIR certification timeline may conflict with development schedule
Pharmacy module integration with external e-prescribing networks adds third-party dependency

phases.name: Parallel Run & Validation
objectives: Run legacy IBMi and modernized system in parallel with synchronized data
Validate output equivalence across all migrated modules
Conduct user acceptance testing with clinical staff
Perform load testing simulating peak census and end-of-day batch volumes
deliverables: Parallel run data synchronization bridge (bidirectional)
Output comparison reports for all migrated transactions
UAT sign-off documentation from clinical department heads
Load test results with capacity planning recommendations
success_criteria: 99.95% transaction equivalence between legacy and modern systems over 4-week parallel run
UAT pass rate exceeds 95% across all clinical workflows
System sustains 2x peak load with p99 latency under 500ms
Zero critical data discrepancies in patient-facing fields
dependencies: P-003 patient registration stable in production
risks: Parallel run may reveal edge cases not captured in business rules catalog
Staff resistance to new system during UAT may delay sign-off
Data synchronization bridge may introduce latency affecting real-time workflows

phases.name: Cutover & Remaining Modules
objectives: Execute phased cutover from IBMi to modernized platform by department
Migrate remaining administrative modules (billing, HR, reporting)
Decommission legacy green screen terminals per department cutover schedule
Execute final data migration with reconciliation and audit trail
deliverables: Department-by-department cutover runbooks
Billing and administrative modules in production
Legacy system decommission certificates per department
Final data reconciliation report with audit trail
success_criteria: Each department cutover completes within designated maintenance window
Billing module processes claims with zero regression vs. legacy
All legacy data migrated with full audit trail and chain of custody
Rollback exercised successfully in pre-production before each department cutover
risks: Cutover window may be insufficient for departments with high transaction volumes
Remaining administrative modules may have undiscovered IBMi dependencies
Staff availability during cutover weekends may be limited

phases.name: Stabilization & Optimization
objectives: Monitor production system stability with enhanced observability
Optimize performance based on real-world usage patterns
Conduct hypercare support with rapid incident response
Complete knowledge transfer to client operations team
deliverables: Production stability report with SLA compliance metrics
Performance optimization recommendations and implementations
Operations runbook and incident response playbook
Knowledge transfer completion certificates for client IT staff
success_criteria: System availability exceeds 99.9% over 4-week stabilization window
Mean time to resolution for P1 incidents under 30 minutes
Client operations team independently resolves L1/L2 incidents
All legacy IBMi systems fully decommissioned
risks: Post-cutover defects may overwhelm hypercare support capacity
Client staff may require extended training beyond planned knowledge transfer

decision_gates.name: MVP Readiness Gate
criteria: Patient Registration MVP passes all functional tests
IAM roles validated against legacy access control matrix
Security penetration test completed with no critical findings

decision_gates.name: Parallel Run Go/No-Go
criteria: UAT sign-off from all department heads
Load test confirms capacity for 2x peak
No unresolved P1 or P2 defects

decision_gates.name: Legacy Decommission Gate
criteria: All departments operating on modernized platform for minimum 2 weeks
Client operations team passed competency assessment
Data retention and archival requirements met for legacy systems
decision_options: Maintain legacy in read-only mode 90 days

sprints.name: Sprint 1: Legacy Discovery
goals: Complete RPG program inventory for patient-facing subsystems
Conduct initial SME interviews for scheduling and registration

sprints.name: Sprint 2: Deep Dive & Data Mapping
goals: Complete data flow mapping for HL7 and X12 interfaces
Document business rules for claims processing and pharmacy modules

sprints.name: Sprint 3: Infrastructure Bootstrap
goals: Establish CI/CD pipeline with container registry

sprints.name: Sprint 4: Infrastructure Hardening
goals: Complete database provisioning with encryption and backup policies
Deploy observability stack and configure alerting thresholds

sprints.name: Sprint 5: IAM Foundation
goals: Implement core IAM service with OIDC/SAML integration
Map legacy IBMi user profiles to RBAC roles

sprints.name: Sprint 6: Patient Registration Core
goals: Build patient registration API with FHIR R4 Patient resource
Implement data migration pipeline for patient demographics

sprints.name: Sprint 7: Registration Completion & Validation
goals: Complete patient search, merge/unmerge, and duplicate detection
Run data migration dry run with accuracy validation

sprints.name: Sprint 8: Scheduling Module
goals: Build scheduling service with provider calendar management

critical_path: P-003: IAM & Patient Registration MVP (blocked by P-001 + P-002)
P-004: Clinical Modules Development (blocked by P-003)
P-005: Parallel Run & Validation (blocked by P-004)
P-006: Cutover & Remaining Modules (blocked by P-005 gate)

risk_register.description: Loss of IBMi tribal knowledge due to retiring developers
risk_register.mitigation: Accelerate knowledge transfer in P-001; record all sessions; pair retiring devs with modern engineers

risk_register.description: HIPAA compliance gaps during migration transition period
risk_register.mitigation: Engage compliance officer from week 1; conduct HIPAA gap analysis during P-002; maintain encryption in transit and at rest throughout

risk_register.description: Data quality issues in legacy DB2/400 tables surface during migration
risk_register.mitigation: Run data profiling in P-001; build data cleansing pipeline; establish data quality thresholds with business stakeholders

risk_register.description: Third-party healthcare system integrations (labs, pharmacies, payers) break during cutover
risk_register.mitigation: Map all external interfaces in P-001; coordinate with third parties in P-004; test integrations during parallel run

risk_register.description: Clinical staff resistance to new system slows UAT and adoption
risk_register.mitigation: Involve clinical champions from P-003; provide hands-on training starting P-004; ensure new UX reduces clicks vs. green screen

risk_register.description: RPG business logic contains undocumented edge cases that cause feature parity gaps
risk_register.mitigation: Use automated RPG code analysis tools in P-001; validate all business rules with SMEs; run extensive parallel comparison in P-005

risk_register.description: Clinical modules phase (P-004) exceeds planned 24-week duration
risk_register.mitigation: Prioritize modules by clinical impact; use MVP scope for each module; maintain 3-week schedule buffer in parallel run phase

external.dependency: HIPAA compliance pre-assessment from external auditor
HL7 FHIR endpoint credentials from connected lab systems
Legacy IBMi source code repository access and documentation

communication_plan.stakeholder_updates: Bi-weekly steering committee briefing with written status report; weekly technical leads stand-up; monthly board-level executive summary
demo_schedule: Week 4: Legacy assessment findings and migration roadmap presentation
Week 10: Infrastructure walkthrough and security architecture review
Week 18: Patient Registration MVP demo to clinical leadership
Week 28: Scheduling and Order Entry modules demo
Week 40: Full clinical suite demo and parallel run readiness review
Week 52: Parallel run results and cutover go/no-go presentation
Week 62: Post-cutover status and stabilization metrics review
Week 68: Final project closeout and lessons learned

decision_points.decision: MVP Readiness: proceed to clinical modules or extend MVP
Parallel Run Go/No-Go: proceed to department cutovers
Legacy Decommission: approve final IBMi shutdown

_migration_specific.legacy_system: IBM AS/400 (IBMi) with RPG IV/ILE programs, CL scripts, DDS-defined display files, and DB2/400 database; 5250 green screen terminal interface serving 350+ concurrent clinical users
_migration_specific.migration_strategy: Strangler Fig pattern with module-by-module replacement; legacy IBMi remains operational while modernized services progressively assume workloads; bidirectional data sync bridge maintains consistency during transition

data_migration_phases.name: Patient Demographics & Master Data
data_migration_phases.approach: ETL with data quality cleansing and deduplication

data_migration_phases.name: Clinical History & Orders
data_migration_phases.approach: Incremental migration with FHIR resource mapping

data_migration_phases.name: Archival & Historical Data
data_migration_phases.approach: Cold storage migration with indexed search capability

_migration_specific.rollback_plan: Each department cutover has a dedicated rollback runbook. Bidirectional sync bridge remains active for 2 weeks post-cutover per department. Rollback triggers: >5 P1 incidents in 24 hours, data integrity failure, or clinical safety concern. Full rollback to IBMi restores green screen access within 4-hour maintenance window. Legacy IBMi maintained in warm standby until DG-003 legacy decommission gate is passed.
feature_parity_checklist: Provider and resource scheduling with recurring appointments
Computerized provider order entry (CPOE) with clinical decision support
Lab order and result interfaces (HL7 v2 and FHIR R4)
Accounts receivable and patient billing statements
Compliance reporting (CMS, state Medicaid, quality measures)
User access control with audit logging equivalent to legacy profiles
